elements.text: ■ No FHA Mortgage may have an original principal balance in excess of the maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal amount as determined under the National Housing Act or in excess of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum loan amounts for conventional Mortgages in Section 4203.1(c), whichever is
elements.label: text

elements.text: less
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original principal balance of a VA Mortgage may not exceed the maximum loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount for a conventional 1-unit Home Mortgage as described in Section 4203.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Mortgage must be covered by a paid-up Mortgage title insurance policy written by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: title insurer and in a form acceptable to the Secretary of HUD or the VA Administrator,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable. Such policy may not be subject to any exceptions other than those
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously approved by the Secretary of HUD or the VA Administrator. The protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: and benefits of such policy in the amounts required by HUD and the VA must run to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and to the Secretary of HUD or the VA Administrator, as applicable. With
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to any FHA Mortgage on a property formerly held by HUD, a title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy is not required if the Secretary of HUD is obligated to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Waive any objection to title by reason of any lien or other adverse interest that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: senior to the FHA Mortgage on the date such FHA Mortgage was filed for record, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Accept an assignment of the FHA Mortgage if the mortgagee is unable to complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure because of a defect in the FHA Mortgage instrument, the FHA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction or the title that existed at or before the time the FHA Mortgage was filed
elements.label: text

elements.text: for record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior encumbrances on the Mortgaged Premises are not acceptable unless such encumbrances
elements.label: text

elements.text: were previously approved by the FHA or VA and, as determined by Freddie Mac, do not
elements.label: text

elements.text: detract from the value of the Mortgaged Premises or affect the First Lien priority or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability of the Mortgage or the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding any requirements applicable to Mortgages generally under the Guide, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following classes of requirements do not apply to FHA or VA Mortgages offered for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Owner-occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Mortgages must be originated on Mortgage instruments approved by the FHA or VA, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the modification made in this section with respect to the document delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide, the Seller must submit such other documents as Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for any FHA or VA Mortgage delivered pursuant to the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage file requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the documentation specified in the Guide, the Mortgage file retained by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller for FHA and VA Mortgages must contain the following documents:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage application of the original Borrower and, if available and applicable, that
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the current Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The credit report on the original Borrower and, if available and applicable, on the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original FHA or VA commitment and, if available and applicable, the FHA Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Commitment Certificate and related documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For VA Mortgages, the VA Certificate of Reasonable Value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The FHA Mortgage Insurance Certificate or VA Loan Guaranty Certificate, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other documents that are required to be retained by federal or State laws, statutes or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Special Seller warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each FHA or VA Mortgage, the Seller further represents and warrants that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The FHA insurance or the VA guaranty is in full force and effect as of the Delivery Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has complied and will continue to comply with all applicable FHA and/or VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: guidelines, regulations and requirements, including, but not limited to, those relating to
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan origination and Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.12 for delivery and pooling requirements for FHA and VA Mortgages sold
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, for Credit Fees related to FHA and VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee provisions stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4205.5: Mortgage previously sold to the Government
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: National Mortgage Association (GNMA) (04/02/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage previously sold to the Government National Mortgage Association (GNMA) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchased from GNMA, or a Mortgage on which a GNMA price differential has been paid, is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, provided it meets all of the requirements of the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents at the time it is offered for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4301: Refinance Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4301.1: Refinance Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase refinance Mortgages under the terms of the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: including this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A refinance Mortgage is either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage, the proceeds of which are used to pay off an existing Mortgage or Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by the Mortgaged Premises with the cancelation of the existing promissory note(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the execution of a new promissory note and Security Instrument, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage secured by Mortgaged Premises previously owned free and clear by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchases the following types of refinance Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A “no cash-out” refinance, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Refi Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A cash-out refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A special purpose cash-out refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Mortgage the proceeds of which are used to pay off an Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: must meet requirements of Chapter 4602 for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Mortgages, including, but not limited to, how the transaction type (purchase or
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance) is determined.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.2: Borrower requirements for refinance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (12/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When an existing Mortgage will be satisfied through a refinance transaction, at least one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on the refinance Mortgage must be a Borrower on the Mortgage being refinanced as
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: When no Borrower on the refinance Mortgage is a Borrower on the Mortgage being refinanced,
elements.label: text

elements.text: one of the following must apply and be documented in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one Borrower on the refinance Mortgage held title to and resided in the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises as a Primary Residence and has been making timely Mortgage payments, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payments for any secondary financing, for the most recent 12-month period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one Borrower on the refinance Mortgage inherited or was legally awarded the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in accordance with a final judgment or decision from a legal body (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: court, jury, judge or arbitrator) such as in a case of divorce, separation or dissolution of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: domestic partnership
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Living Trust may be made irrevocable by a Settlor’s death. To be an eligible Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time of the refinance transaction, the Borrower must continue to be a Living Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: that meets Freddie Mac’s revocability and, as applicable, other eligibility requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.3: Refinance practices (12/03/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowable refinance practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unacceptable refinance practices and remedies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prepayment speed monitoring and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Allowable refinance practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Present refinance or payoff information to any Borrower who requests such information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct broad-based refinance advertising, telephone or other campaigns directed at
elements.label: text

elements.text: broad categories of Borrowers, such as those with Mortgages in their Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: portfolio, Borrowers with Mortgage coupons above a certain level, certain Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: products (e.g., conventional fixed-rate Mortgages or ARMs) or Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in particular geographic areas
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Unacceptable refinance practices and remedies
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer may not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deliver any Mortgage to Freddie Mac obtained from a Mortgage Broker or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correspondent if the Seller has knowledge or reason to believe that the Mortgage Broker
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Correspondent has received an application to refinance or has agreed to refinance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage (even if the agreement is not in writing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell or deliver a Mortgage to Freddie Mac if the Seller has knowledge or reason to
elements.label: text

elements.text: believe that the Borrower has entered into, or has agreed to enter into, a refinancing
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangement (even if the agreement is not in writing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell or deliver a Mortgage to Freddie Mac without full and accurate disclosure of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: material information about the Mortgage (see Sections 4201.1, 4201.10, 6201.6(a) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8101.4(d)). Any information related to refinancing or proclivity for refinancing is
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered material information to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Originate a cash-out refinance Mortgage or a purchase transaction Mortgage with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: intention of refinancing that Mortgage as a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale to Freddie Mac. The “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage is ineligible for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. See Section 4301.4 for requirements related to the required age of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage being refinanced when the subject transaction is a “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as stated in Section 4302.2 for Refi Possible® Mortgages, in advertising or
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementing refinance terms, a Seller/Servicer may not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Intentionally target Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Segregate Mortgages in its own portfolio from those sold to Freddie Mac for different
elements.label: text

elements.text: treatment in terms of refinance advertising, offers or practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must incorporate adequate controls in its origination and refinancing
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures to prevent unacceptable refinance practices by the Seller/Servicer or any of its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Brokers and Correspondents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer that (i) engages in unacceptable refinance practices, (ii) knowingly sells or
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivers Mortgages to Freddie Mac from Mortgage Brokers or Correspondents it knew, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: should have known, were engaging in unacceptable refinance practices, or (iii) fails to
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain proper controls for such Mortgages being sold or delivered to Freddie Mac will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to any or all of the remedies available to Freddie Mac at law or in equity and pursuant
elements.label: text

elements.text: to this Guide and relevant Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Those remedies include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disqualification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suspension, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requiring the Seller/Servicer to make Freddie Mac whole for losses, including losses
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with repurchases at par for Mortgages purchased at premium prices and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses associated with claims made by security investors. With respect to claims by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: investors, the disposition of such claims is solely within the discretion of Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer that has any questions about compliance with Freddie Mac requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: should contact its Freddie Mac account manager (or other designated Freddie Mac personnel)
elements.label: text

elements.text: to ensure compliance with Freddie Mac’s requirements and to facilitate full and accurate
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosure of all pertinent information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Prepayment speed monitoring and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects the Seller/Servicer to monitor the prepayment levels of its Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: particularly refinance Mortgages. If the Seller/Servicer becomes aware of circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: likely to result in unusually high prepayment rates on Mortgages purchased from it by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, it must notify its Freddie Mac account manager immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If requested to do so by Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer is obligated to cooperate fully and
elements.label: text

elements.text: promptly with Freddie Mac personnel and to provide adequate information in trying to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the reason and a solution for any such high prepayment rates. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: reserves the right to initiate on its own an investigation of high prepayment rates of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: particular Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.4: “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage is a Mortgage for which the proceeds may be used only as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage must meet the Borrower requirements in Section 4301.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowable uses of proceeds from a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowable uses of proceeds for Refi Possible® Mortgages, CHOICERenovation® Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: GreenCHOICE Mortgages®, Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Treatment of excess proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Allowable uses of proceeds from a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Proceeds from a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage may be used only to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off the principal and interest due, which may include the payoff of a balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred under a loss mitigation plan, for the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First Mortgage, regardless of its age, provided the first Mortgage was used to acquire
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property, regardless of the age of the first Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First Mortgage, originated as a refinance transaction, with a Note Date no less than 30
elements.label: text

elements.text: days prior to the Note Date of the “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage, as documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file (e.g., on the credit report or the title commitment)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off any costs or fees associated with the satisfaction and release of the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage (e.g., late fees, prepayment penalties, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off or pay down any junior liens secured by the Mortgaged Premises that were
elements.label: text

elements.text: used in their entirety to acquire the subject property. Any remaining balance must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinated to the refinance Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower is not required to satisfy outstanding junior liens secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, provided that the junior lien meets the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing in Section 4204.1 and/or Affordable Seconds® in Section 4204.2,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If a junior lien was paid off as part of the “no cash-out” refinance transaction, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must maintain documentation in the Mortgage file demonstrating that the full
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of the lien was used for the purchase of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay any required share of appreciation due to the subsidy provider per the resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted covenants, subject to the requirements in Section 4406.8(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay related Closing Costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Real estate taxes that are past due and/or delinquent, as defined by the taxing
elements.label: text

elements.text: authority, may not be paid with the proceeds of the “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except that if the transaction results in cash out as permitted in the following bullet, these
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds may be used to pay the delinquent taxes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disburse cash out to the Borrower (or any other payee) up to the greater of 1% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: new refinance Mortgage or $2,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off the outstanding balance of a land contract or contract for deed if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements from Section 4404.1 are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the executed land contract or contract for deed must be included in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The land contract or contract for deed must have been executed at least 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the Application Received Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage file must include third-party documentation evidencing payments in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the land contract or contract for deed for the most recent 12-month
elements.label: text

elements.text: period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The loan-to-value ratio must be calculated using the current appraised value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off a Property Assessed Clean Energy (PACE) or PACE-like obligation, subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the additional requirements in Section 4301.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Allowable uses of proceeds for Refi Possible Mortgages, CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, GreenCHOICE Mortgages, Construction to Permanent Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Refi Possible Mortgages, see the limitations on the uses of proceeds in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For CHOICERenovation Mortgages, proceeds may be used to pay off the existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage debt and/or finance the eligible renovations as described in Section 4607.6,
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to the additional requirements in Sections 4607.7(c) and 4607.7(d), as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For GreenCHOICE Mortgages, proceeds may be used to pay an Existing Debt, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 4606.1(c), subject to the additional requirements in Section 4606.3(b),
elements.label: text

elements.text: and to finance eligible improvements as described in Section 4606.1(b), subject to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional requirements in Section 4606.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages, see Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c)Treatment of excess proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are remaining proceeds from the “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage after the proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: are applied as described above:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage amount must be reduced, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The excess amount must be applied as a principal curtailment to the new refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage at closing and must be clearly reflected on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement. See Section 6302.32 for special delivery requirements for Mortgages with
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal curtailments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under no circumstances may cash disbursed to the Borrower (or any other payee) exceed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum permitted for “no cash-out” refinance Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.5: Cash-out refinance Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A cash-out refinance Mortgage is a Mortgage for which there are no specific restrictions on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: use of the proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupancy requirement for Primary Residences
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Six-month ownership requirement for the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-out refinance Mortgage paying off a First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-out refinance Mortgage on a property owned free and clear
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Occupancy requirement for Primary Residences
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a cash-out refinance Mortgage secured by a Primary Residence, all Borrowers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupy the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Six-month ownership requirement for the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all cash-out refinance Mortgages, at least one Borrower must have been on title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises for at least six months prior to the Note Date or as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estates: When the property is a leasehold estate, at least one Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have been lessee on the ground lease or lease agreement of the subject leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: for at least six months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Unit: When the property is a Cooperative Unit, at least one Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have held Cooperative Shares corresponding to the Cooperative Unit that is the subject of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Share Loan for at least six months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title held by limited liability company (LLC) or limited partnership (LP): When title
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the property is held by an LLC or LP, the time the property was titled in the name of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the LLC or LP may be included in the six-month requirement provided:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ At least one Borrower must have been the majority owner or had control of the LLC
elements.label: text

elements.text: or LP since the date the property was acquired by the LLC or LP, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Title must be transferred from the LLC or LP into the Borrower’s name on or before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgages must comply with the ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement for the Mortgaged Premises in Section 4301.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: If none of the Borrowers have been on title to the Mortgaged Premises for at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: six months prior to the Note Date of the cash-out refinance Mortgage, the requirement(s) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following table must be met and documented in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions to the six-months on title requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scenario Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inheritance or legal award At least one Borrower on the refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage inherited or was legally awarded the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in accordance with a final
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment or decision from a legal body (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: court, jury, judge or arbitrator) such as in a case
elements.label: text

elements.text: of divorce, separation or dissolution of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: domestic partnership
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delayed financing For delayed financing, all of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Settlement/Closing Disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement or an alternative form required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: law from the purchase transaction must
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflect that no financing secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property was used to purchase the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property. A recorded trustee’s deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: or equivalent documentation may be used
elements.label: text

elements.text: when a Settlement/Closing Disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement or an alternative form required by

elements.text: (c) Cash-out refinance Mortgage paying off a First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a cash-out refinance Mortgage is used to pay off an existing First Lien Mortgage, the

elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, the First Lien Mortgage being refinanced must be seasoned for at least 12

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The First Lien Mortgage being refinanced is a Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The cash-out refinance Mortgage is a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purpose of the cash-out refinance Mortgage is to convert the Manufactured Home to
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally classified real property under applicable State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Cash-out refinance Mortgage on a property owned free and clear

elements.text: 4301.6: Special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgage is a cash-out refinance Mortgage where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: owner of a property uses the proceeds to buy out the equity of a co-owner.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgage must meet the loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratio requirements for cash-out
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgages in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 5703.8(a) for Mortgages secured by a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 4203.1(b) for all other Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgage must meet all requirements of the Guide that
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to cash-out refinance Mortgages, except as specifically modified below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowable uses of proceeds from a special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Borrower requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Allowable uses of proceeds from a special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan amount of a special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgage is limited to amounts used
elements.label: text

elements.text: to buy out the equity of the co-owner, which may include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Paying off the first Mortgage, regardless of age
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Paying off junior liens secured by the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Borrower is not required to satisfy outstanding junior liens secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises if the requirements of Section 4204.1 for Mortgages with secondary
elements.label: text

elements.text: financing and/or Section 4204.2 for Affordable Seconds®, are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Paying related Closing Costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special Borrower requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower and the co-owner receiving the buy-out proceeds must have jointly owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property for a minimum of 12 months prior to the initial loan application. (Parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: who inherited an interest in the property are exempt from this requirement.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower and the co-owner receiving the buy-out proceeds must provide evidence
elements.label: text

elements.text: that they occupied the subject property as their Primary Residence. (Parties who inherited
elements.label: text

elements.text: an interest in the property are exempt from this requirement.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower and the co-owner receiving the buy-out proceeds must provide a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement, signed by all parties, stating the property transfer terms of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer and disposition of the refinance proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower who retains sole ownership of the property may not receive any of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds from the refinance transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must retain the following in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation evidencing that the Borrower and the co-owner jointly occupied the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises as their Primary Residence, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the written agreement stating the terms of property transfer and the disposition
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the refinance proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, for Credit Fees related to special purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: cash-out refinance Mortgages. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions stated in Chapter 6303.

elements.text: (a) General requirements for Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The generally accepted commercial terms used to describe Mortgages originated under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Article XVI of the Texas Constitution (“cash-out refinance”, “rate-term refinance”) may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: correspond to the meaning given the same or comparable terms when used in Chapter 4301.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers must understand the distinctions between Freddie Mac’s refinance definitions in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4301 and the provisions of Section 50(a)(6) and are responsible for determining:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When Section 50(a)(6) applies, regardless of the definitions of cash-out and “no cash out”
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance transactions used in Chapter 4301
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the proposed refinance of a Mortgage secured by the Borrower’s homestead in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the State of Texas is a Mortgage that must be originated pursuant to Section 50(a)(6) of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Article XVI of the Texas Constitution
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A conventional First Lien Mortgage that is a:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Fixed-rate Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ 3/6-Month, 5/6-Month, 7/6-Month or 10/6-Month ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A cash-out refinance Mortgage as described in Section 4301.5 or a “no cash-out”
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgage as described in Section 4301.4, depending on the applicable facts
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage may not be a special purpose cash-out refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Refinances of Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may refinance a Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage into:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Another Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A refinance Mortgage permitted by Section 50(a)(4) of Article XVI of the Texas
elements.label: text

elements.text: Constitution (a “Section 50(a)(4) Mortgage”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Section 50(a)(4) Mortgages used to pay off a Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and meeting all applicable Texas Constitutional requirements are not subject to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section and are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as “no cash-out”
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgages, provided all requirements of Section 4301.4 are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Mortgaged Premises; appraisal; fair market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Mortgage must be secured by a Mortgaged Premises that is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 1-unit Primary Residence,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Located in the State of Texas, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s homestead
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Living Trust that meets the eligibility requirements of Section 5103.5 may be a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage if the Living Trust also meets the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a “qualifying trust” under Texas law for purposes of owning residential property that
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifies for the homestead exemption.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must provide an appraisal that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Meets Freddie Mac requirements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complies with Section 50(a)(6)(Q)(ix) and Section 50(h) of Article XVI of the Texas
elements.label: text

elements.text: Constitution
elements.label: text

elements.text: To meet the requirements for acknowledgment of fair market value, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Along with the owner of the homestead, execute a written acknowledgment of the “fair
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value” of the homestead property as of the date the extension of credit is made
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attach the appraisal report to the acknowledgment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) LTV and TLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum LTV and TLTV ratios for Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed 80% and must be lower if necessary to comply with the LTV/TLTV ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Sections 4203.1(b) and 4301.4 for “no cash out” and cash out refinances, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Eligibility to sell and service Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise notified in writing, Sellers are eligible to deliver Texas Equity Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 50(a)(6) Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must be eligible to service Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8104.1(a) and related Servicing provisions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation that results in or provides for First Lien priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4302.2: Acceptable refinance practices (12/04/24)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advertising and solicitation

elements.text: ■ Advertising and other communications with Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Advertising and solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may target Mortgages owned or securitized by Freddie Mac for the Refi Possible®
elements.label: text

elements.text: offering, provided that the Borrower’s income is less than or equal to 100% of the Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: Median Income and the Seller simultaneously applies the same advertising and solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: activities with respect to Borrowers with Mortgages owned or securitized by Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Advertising and other communications with Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller chooses to reach out to Borrowers and the Seller’s communication includes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reference to Freddie Mac, then the communication must also include the following (or
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 12 months

elements.text: the temporary eligibility requirements in Bulletin 2020-17, those missed payments

elements.text: requirements.

elements.text: A Refi Possible® Mortgage that is a refinance of a Mortgage with recourse or indemnification

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is delivered with recourse or indemnification; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: If... Then...

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage being refinanced was

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4305: Purchase Transaction Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4305.1: General requirements for all purchase
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: transaction Mortgages (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase transaction definition
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overpayment of costs, fees and charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gifts funds or gift of equity exceeding the amount needed for closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Required documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Impact of cash back on minimum Borrower contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Purchase transaction definition
elements.label: text

elements.text: A purchase transaction Mortgage is a Mortgage for which the proceeds are used for one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acquire the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acquire the Mortgaged Premises and finance improvements to the property as permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Seller’s Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off the Interim Construction Financing in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4602 for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off the outstanding balance under a land contract or contract for deed in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with requirements of Chapter 4404
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Overpayment of costs, fees and charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower may receive cash back, or a principal curtailment may be made, as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for the overpayment of costs, fees and charges paid by the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of such overpayments may include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An earnest money deposit exceeding the required Down Payment amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fee paid at loan application that is covered by a financing concession (as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5501.6(b)) at loan closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Closing Cost that is reduced after closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Borrower may receive cash back, or a principal curtailment may be made,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a result of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In jurisdictions where real estate taxes are paid in arrears, receipt of funds from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property seller for real estate taxes that cover a period prior to the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refunds mandated by federal laws or regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Gift funds or gift of equity exceeding the amount needed for closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gift funds exceeding the amount needed for closing may be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disbursed to the Borrower as cash back, which includes pay off or pay down of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s debt, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applied as a principal curtailment
elements.label: text

elements.text: A gift of equity exceeding the amount needed for closing may only be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Used to pay off or pay down the Borrower’s debt at closing when paid by the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: seller to the creditor, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applied as a principal curtailment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Required documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any cash back, pay off or pay down of debt or principal curtailment, as described above,
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be reflected on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement. In instances of
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for the overpayment of costs, fees and charges, and/or refunds mandated by
elements.label: text

elements.text: federal law or regulation, the Mortgage file must include documentation supporting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount and the reason for the reimbursement and/or refund.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Impact of cash back on minimum Borrower contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum Borrower contribution, if applicable, must be met at closing as described in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 5501.3(k) for certain Mortgages secured by second homes, Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Properties and Mortgages with shared equity plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 4501.7 for Home Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Section 4504.7(a) for HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the projected cash back, as described above, results in the Borrower not meeting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum Borrower contribution at closing, the excess amount of the cash back must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: applied as a principal curtailment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4401: ARMs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4401.1: General ARM eligibility requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible ARM products
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Common requirements for all ARM products
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ARM Note Rate change requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible ARM products
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase rate-capped ARMs that have an Initial Period followed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent Note Rate adjustments. The following table shows, for each eligible ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: product, the applicable Initial Period and the subsequent periodic adjustments:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the Initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3/6-Month ARM 36 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6 months 5/6-Month ARM 60 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7/6-Month ARM 84 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10/6-Month ARM 120 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4401.1(c) for first Interest Change Date requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only an ARM with an Initial Period of 5, 7 or 10 years is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Higher-Priced Mortgage Loan and Higher-Priced Covered Transaction. See the Glossary
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Section 4202.2(b) for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Exhibit 17S, Available Mortgage Products, provides a consolidated summary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: certain information contained in this chapter regarding eligible ARM products.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Common requirements for all ARM products
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Index
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs are subject to periodic Note Rate adjustments based on the value of an Index at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified time, as set forth in the Note. Only ARMs that use the 30-day Average SOFR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Index are eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Lookback Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: The time interval for establishing the Index value is referred to as the Lookback Period,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and all eligible ARM products have a 45-day Lookback Period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Due Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Due Date must occur on the first day of the month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Payment Change Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Payment Change Date must occur on the first day of the month following each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Change Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Calculation of monthly payment amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: The monthly payment amount that is calculated following each Interest Change Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be sufficient to amortize the UPB fully over the remaining term of the ARM.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Margin
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Margin must be equal to or greater than 100 basis points and less than or equal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: 300 basis points.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii)Lifetime Ceiling
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Lifetime Ceiling must equal the sum of the Note Rate at origination plus the Life
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cap stated in the Note, as described in Section 4401.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Lifetime Floor
elements.label: text

elements.text: For ARMs with Lifetime Floors, the Lifetime Floor must equal the Margin stated in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note, as specified in Section 4401.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ix)Title insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must provide title insurance in accordance with Chapter 4702.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) ARM Note Rate change requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac must comply with the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section regarding the timing of, and Periodic Caps for, Interest Change Dates and how Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate adjustments are made and disclosed. Note Rate adjustments must comply with the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Note and with applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Interest Change Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Interest Change Date, stated in the Note, must occur on the first day of the month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the first Interest Change Date, subsequent interest changes occur every 6 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table shows when the first Interest Change Date must occur:

elements.text: (ii) Calculation of adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 8502.2 for requirements related to determining Note Rate changes on

elements.text: ARMs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Limits on Note Rate adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each ARM Note must specify limits on the periodic adjustments (the Lifetime Ceiling,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Lifetime Floor and the Periodic Cap) to the Note Rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Note Rate may not exceed the Lifetime Ceiling or be less than the Lifetime Floor,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must equal the Margin as required by Section 4401.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Note Rate at the first Interest Change Date may not exceed the value of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate at origination plus the Initial Cap or be less than the value of the Note Rate at
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination minus the Initial Cap.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustments on subsequent Interest Change Dates are subject to the Periodic Cap.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Applicable caps for eligible ARM products
elements.label: text

elements.text: All eligible ARM products have (in percentage points) a Periodic Cap of 1% and a Life
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cap of 5%.

elements.label: text

elements.text: 4402.1: Common requirements for Seller-Owned
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Converted Mortgages and Seller-Owned Modified
