elements.text: Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property value warranty requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium, leasehold estate and Planned Unit Development (PUD) warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Requirements related to the Mortgage prior to conversion or modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to conversion or modification, the Mortgage must have had the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must have been secured by a First Lien on a 1- to 4-unit Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence; if the Mortgage is a Home Possible® Mortgage it must have been secured
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The occupancy type of the Mortgaged Premises must not have changed since the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date or since the Effective Date of Permanent Financing if the Mortgage was
elements.label: text

elements.text: originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage met all Freddie Mac’s eligibility and underwriting requirements on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date or on the Effective Date of Permanent Financing if the Mortgage was
elements.label: text

elements.text: originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The loan-to-value (LTV), total LTV (TLTV) and Home Equity Line of Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratios did not exceed the limits in Section 4203.1(b) or,
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Home Possible Mortgages, Section 4501.7 as of the Note Date or as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Permanent Financing if the Mortgage was originated as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The principal balance of the Mortgage has not increased since the Note Date or since
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Effective Date of Permanent Financing if the Mortgage was originated as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage, and the loan amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage prior to modification or conversion did not exceed the maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan amount in Section 4203.1(c) in effect on the Note Date. The Mortgage may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be a super conforming Mortgage (as described in Chapter 4603).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was not in default and meets the requirements in Section 4201.6
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was a conventional, fully amortizing Mortgage, with an original
elements.label: text

elements.text: amortization term no greater than 30 years from the date that is one month before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first payment Due Date as described in Section 4201.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was not an interest-only Mortgage prior to conversion or modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Requirements for the Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage or Seller-Owned Modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage or Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage must have the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following characteristics:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage meets all Freddie Mac’s eligibility and underwriting requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect on the Delivery Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current LTV, TLTV and HTLTV ratios do not exceed the limits in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4203.1(b) or, for Home Possible Mortgages, Section 4501.7 as of the Delivery Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current ratios are calculated by dividing the UPB of the Mortgage as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Date by the value as defined in Section 4203.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must comply with the maximum original loan amounts stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4203.1(c) in effect on the Settlement Date. The Mortgage may not be a super
elements.label: text

elements.text: conforming Mortgage as described in Chapter 4603.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller may not have assessed a prepayment penalty in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversion or modification of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrowers are the same as the Borrowers on the Mortgage prior to conversion or
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification, except that a Borrower who contributed no qualifying income, assets or
elements.label: text

elements.text: reserves may have been removed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is a conventional, fully amortizing Mortgage and the Mortgage term
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not extend beyond 30 years from the date that is one month before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date as described in Section 4201.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is a Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage if all of the following conditions are
elements.label: text

elements.text: met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The terms of the Permanent Financing have been modified or, for an ARM, converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a fixed-rate Mortgage after the Effective Date of Permanent Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage meets the requirements of this Chapter 4402
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is not secured by a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following special underwriting requirements apply to Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are not Home Possible Accept Mortgages and to Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Except as modified by this chapter, the Seller is required to manually underwrite and
elements.label: text

elements.text: requalify each Borrower using Freddie Mac’s eligibility and underwriting requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Topics 5100 through 5500 in effect as of the Delivery Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage or Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage is not eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be submitted to Loan Product Advisor® for assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage or Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive any representation and warranty relief relating to the assessment of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Loan Product Advisor, and any previous relief provided is of no force and effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: in connection with the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following special underwriting requirements apply to all Seller-Owned Converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage or Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: and underwriting requirements must be based on the loan purpose as of the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must comply with the minimum Indicator Score requirements in Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 25, Mortgages with Risk Class and/or Minimum Indicator Score Requirements, for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Mortgage product and must meet the requirements of Topics 5100 through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5500. If no Borrower has a usable Credit Score and, as a result, the Mortgage does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: have an Indicator Score, the Mortgage is not eligible for purchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible Mortgages must be either Manually Underwritten Mortgages or Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor Accept Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must maintain in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All documentation required as of the Note Date or as of the Effective Date of Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing if the Mortgage was originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All documentation associated with the conversion or modification of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payment history of the Mortgage for the 12-month period prior to the Delivery Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or the full length of the payment history, if less than 12 months) documented by a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report or a Servicer-generated payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit, employment and income documentation required to requalify each Borrower in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the Standard Documentation requirements in Topic 5300 for the Seller￾Owned Converted Mortgage or Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ New Uniform Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ New credit report meeting the requirements of Section 5203.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Verification of income and employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: This requirement does not apply to Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are Home Possible Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other documentation required as of the Delivery Date (e.g., the Indicator Score). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac required a minimum Indicator Score for the Mortgage at time of origination,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must note this Indicator Score in addition to the Indicator Score required for
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery under this chapter on Form 1077.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwriting documentation must be dated no more than 120 days prior to the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Conversion Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Property value warranty requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements must be met regarding the valuation of the Mortgaged Premises:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must provide a new appraisal with an effective date no more than 120 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the modification or Conversion Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal must meet Freddie Mac requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller warrants that the property value has not declined since the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent appraisal if the Mortgage was originated as a Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The new appraisal must not be used to determine the original LTV, TLTV and HTLTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratios for the Mortgage or the current LTV, TLTV and HTLTV ratios as of the Delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Condominium, leasehold estate and Planned Unit Development (PUD) warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by Condominium Units, the Seller must underwrite the Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project and, based on such evaluation, represent and warrant that the Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the requirements in Chapter 5701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by leasehold estates must meet the special warranties in Chapter 5704 as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by PUDs must meet the special warranties in Chapter 5702 as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Mortgage insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are mortgage insurance requirements for Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Mortgage that had an LTV ratio greater than 80% on the Note Date or on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date of Permanent Financing if the Mortgage was originated as a Construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage and has a current LTV ratio greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: 80%, the required level of mortgage insurance coverage will be determined by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in the Guide as of the Note Date or as of the Effective Date of Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing if the Mortgage was originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller is not required to increase existing mortgage insurance coverage to comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 4701.1 if Freddie Mac’s minimum coverage amounts have increased
elements.label: text

elements.text: between the (i) Note Date or the Effective Date of Permanent Financing if the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: was originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Delivery Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer must warrant that conversion or modification has not altered or
elements.label: text

elements.text: impaired the coverage under the mortgage insurance policy. If the Seller/Servicer cannot
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrant that the insurance policy has not been impaired, the Seller/Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: an endorsement that brings the policy into compliance with the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender-paid mortgage insurance is permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The custom mortgage insurance option described in Section 4701.1 and financed
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance premiums described in Section 4701.2(a) are not permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Form 1077
elements.label: text

elements.text: On Form 1077, or on another document in the Mortgage file, the Seller must state that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is a Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage or a Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, Seller must state that the Mortgage was originated as a Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must meet the title insurance requirements of Chapter 4702. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must warrant that conversion or modification has not altered or impaired
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage under the title insurance policy or attorney’s opinion of title.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is selected for Freddie Mac’s post-funding quality control, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must provide both the original underwriting file and the underwriting file required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: requalify the Borrower at the time of conversion or modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4402.2: Special requirements for Seller-Owned
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Converted Mortgages (05/07/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are requirements related to Mortgage eligibility for Seller-Owned Converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Community Land Trust Mortgage is not eligible for sale as a Seller-Owned Converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A HeritageOne® Mortgage is not eligible for sale as a Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage secured by property subject to income-based resale restrictions that
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate upon foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure where the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: value must be determined in accordance with Section 4406.4(c) is not eligible for sale as
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage with a temporary subsidy buydown plan is not eligible for sale as a Seller￾Owned Converted Mortgage. If the original Mortgage included a temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown, any remaining funds in the buydown account at time of conversion must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: distributed in accordance with the buydown agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must warrant that all the original Note and Security Instrument terms for
elements.label: text

elements.text: converting the Mortgage to a fixed rate of interest have been met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage Note must be negotiable, and the entire
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness must be fully enforceable against the Borrower and secured by a First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage Note must have a fixed rate of interest with level
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly principal and interest payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fully enforceable due-on-sale clause, such as contained in Freddie Mac’s current fixed￾rate Uniform Instruments, must be in effect after conversion to fixed rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.19 for delivery and pooling requirements for Seller-Owned Converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4402.3: Uniform Instruments and nonstandard
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: documents for Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original Security Instrument and Convertible Adjustable Rate Rider
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original Convertible Adjustable Rate Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original convertible ARM may have been originated using Uniform Instruments in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 4101.2 or nonstandard documents. Documentation for the Seller-Owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Converted Mortgage should consist of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original Security Instrument and Convertible Adjustable Rate Rider; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A document evidencing the conversion of the ARM to a fixed-rate Mortgage; the conversion
elements.label: text

elements.text: document must be recorded if necessary to establish that the Seller-Owned Converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is a First Lien. The Seller may use the Freddie Mac Multistate Agreement to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Convert, Freddie Mac Uniform Instrument Form 3180, to evidence the ARM conversion. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller uses a loan instrument other than the Agreement to Convert, the Seller represents
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warrants that the instrument, when completed, contains substantially identical provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Freddie Mac Agreement to Convert and is appropriate for use to evidence the
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversion of the Convertible ARM; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The original Convertible Adjustable Rate Note with the document evidencing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversion attached, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A new fixed-rate Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a new Note is executed, the Seller must use the Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Fixed-Rate Note as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by Section 4101.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage is documented with the original documents and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: document evidencing the ARM conversion, the following requirements apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Original Security Instrument and Convertible Adjustable Rate Rider
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original Security Instrument and Convertible Adjustable Rate Rider must be either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The version of the Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument for the State in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Mortgaged Premises is located in effect on the Note Date, with either the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac Uniform Convertible Adjustable Rate Rider, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A nonstandard document that contains provisions comparable to the provisions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: version of the Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument in effect on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date for the State in which the Mortgaged Premises are located, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions relating to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Default and foreclosure rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The use of hazard insurance proceeds, condemnation and hazardous waste
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No waivers of homestead rights, dower or similar marital rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Matters that affect the security for the Mortgage, including charges and liens and
elements.label: text

elements.text: property preservation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Borrower Mortgage payment and escrow obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Rights of the lender in the event of Borrower bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Original Convertible Adjustable Rate Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original Convertible Adjustable Rate Note must be either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The applicable version of the Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac Uniform Convertible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustable Rate Note in effect on the Note Date, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A nonstandard Convertible Adjustable Rate Note, provided the Convertible Adjustable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate Note contains provisions that are consistent with the provisions in the Fannie Mae or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Convertible Adjustable Rate Note for ARMs originated on the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must review the Convertible Adjustable Rate Note for consistency, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: the provisions relating to the following, and determine that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The late payment fee does not exceed the maximum fee allowed by State law and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ There are no provisions allowing skipped payments, a grace period following a partial
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or a grace period for other hardship or other reasons
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ There are no waivers of homestead rights, dower or similar marital rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages originated as a Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Seller-Owned Converted Mortgage that was originated as a Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage, the conversion of Interim Construction Financing to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permanent Financing must have occurred prior to the conversion from a Convertible ARM to
elements.label: text

elements.text: a fixed-rate Mortgage and must have been documented in accordance with Chapter 4602.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The conversion from a Convertible ARM to a fixed-rate Mortgage must be documented in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4402.4: Special requirements for Seller-Owned
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Modified Mortgages (05/07/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless specifically made applicable to Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages, negotiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting provisions in the Seller’s Purchase Documents do not apply to Seller-Owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modified Mortgages and cannot be used with Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage may not have been originated as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage using an Automated Valuation Model (AVM)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A HomeOne® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Community Land Trust Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A HeritageOne® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage secured by a property subject to income-based resale restrictions that
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate upon foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure where the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: value must be determined in accordance with Section 4406.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must only be modified for the purpose of a reduction in the interest rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.27 for delivery and pooling requirements for Seller-Owned Modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4402.5: Uniform Instruments for Seller-Owned
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Modified Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages originated as a Construction to Permanent Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation for a Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage should consist of all of the following.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Security Instrument must be either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The version of the Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument for the State in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Mortgaged Premises is located in effect on the Note Date, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A nonstandard document that has been modified to conform to the current Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument for the State in which the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises are located
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: The promissory note must be either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Fixed-Rate Note with the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement attached, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A new Note; a new Note must be used if the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage is not a
elements.label: text

elements.text: fixed-rate Mortgage. If a new Note is executed, Seller must use the applicable Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument Note as required by Section 4101.2 for the modified Mortgage Product.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Loan modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan modification agreement must be recorded if necessary to establish that the Seller￾Owned Modified Mortgage is a First Lien.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan modification agreement must contain the following information with respect to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification on the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The effective date of the loan modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current outstanding principal balance on the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the interest rate on the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage is fixed or adjustable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The yearly interest rate on the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The monthly payment amount on the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The date the monthly payments on the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage begin
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maturity date of the Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may use the Freddie Mac Multistate Loan Modification to a Fixed Interest Rate,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Uniform Instrument Form 5161, as the loan modification agreement if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original Mortgage is either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fixed-rate Mortgage originated on the Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Fixed-Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note and after modification will remain a fixed-rate Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is being modified to a fixed-rate Mortgage using the Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Fixed-Rate Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Seller uses a different instrument to evidence the loan modification for a fixed-rate Seller￾Owned Modified Mortgage, the Seller represents and warrants that the instrument, when
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed, contains substantially identical provisions to the Freddie Mac Loan Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a Fixed Interest Rate and is appropriate for use to evidence the modification of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages originated as a Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Seller-Owned Modified Mortgage that was originated as a Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or Renovation Mortgage, the conversion of Interim Construction Financing to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permanent Financing must have occurred prior to the modification and must have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented in accordance with Chapter 4602. The modification of the Permanent Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: that occurs after the Effective Date of Permanent Financing must be documented in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with this Section 4402.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4403: Mortgages with Capitalized
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4403.1: Mortgages with capitalized balances (12/04/24)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages having principal balances that include capitalization of interest, taxes, hazard
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premiums and/or late charges are eligible for purchase under certain conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages are not eligible for sale as Mortgages with capitalized balances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions that terminate
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required foreclosure redemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, where the property value must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 4406.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: By delivery of Mortgages with capitalized balances, the Seller represents and warrants the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements have been met as of the Delivery Date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Capitalization is permitted under the Mortgage documents and applicable State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Note is negotiable, and the entire indebtedness, including all amounts capitalized, is
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully enforceable against the Borrower and secured by a First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The UPB of the Mortgage, including all amounts capitalized, does not exceed the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan amount as reflected in the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. No capitalization will occur after the Purchase Contract Date of Acceptance
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Mortgage is fully amortizing in substantially equal monthly installments of principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and interest over a period of up to 10, 15, 20 or 30 years, respectively (depending on
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Mortgage is to be included in a 10-, 15-, 20- or 30-year Pool) following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Date. If, as a result of the capitalization, the Note maturity date could have
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceeded the maximum term to maturity for the related Pool after delivery to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or could have resulted in a balloon payment at maturity, the Seller must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: reamortized the Mortgage before delivery to Freddie Mac. For each Mortgage that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been reamortized or for which the stated maturity date has been extended, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: makes the following additional representations and warranties:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each affected Borrower has been informed of the capitalization, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If such Borrower’s Mortgage contains a stated maturity date, that capitalization will
elements.label: text

elements.text: result in installments becoming due after such stated maturity date or an increase in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. All computations of maturity dates are correct
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 6302.22 for delivery requirements for Mortgages with capitalized balances.

elements.text: Chapter 4404: Land Contract; Contract for Deed
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4404.1: Land contract; contract for deed (02/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase transaction Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the proceeds of a Mortgage are used to pay the outstanding balance under a land contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: or contract for deed, the Mortgage may be considered either a purchase or a “no cash-out”
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgage if the requirements in this section are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A copy of the executed land contract or contract for deed must be included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Purchase transaction Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the transaction to be considered a purchase transaction:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The land contract or contract for deed must have been executed less than 12 months prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Application Received Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All of the loan proceeds must be used to pay the outstanding balance under the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract or contract for deed and no loan proceeds may be disbursed to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The loan-to-value (LTV) ratio must be calculated using the lesser of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The current appraised value of the Mortgaged Premises, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The total acquisition cost (the purchase price indicated in the original land contract or
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract for deed, plus any cost the Borrower has expended for rehabilitation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovation, refurbishment or energy improvements). The Mortgage file must contain
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient documentation on which to calculate the total acquisition cost.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the transaction to be considered a “no cash-out” refinance transaction:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The land contract or contract for deed must have been executed at least 12 months prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Application Received Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The LTV ratio must be calculated using the current appraised value of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage file must include third-party documentation evidencing payments in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the land contract or contract for deed for the most recent 12-month
elements.label: text

elements.text: period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must meet the requirements for “no cash-out” refinance Mortgages in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4301.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406.2: Resale restriction requirements for Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: secured by properties subject to resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions, there are no limits on the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: period in which the resale restrictions may remain in place on the property. The resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions may be in effect (i.e., survive) for a certain number of years or continue in perpetuity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The resale restrictions must be binding on current and subsequent property owners, and survive
elements.label: text

elements.text: until they are formally removed or modified, or terminate automatically in accordance with their
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon completion of foreclosure, which includes the expiration of any applicable legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: required foreclosure redemption period, or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If necessary, upon recordation of the associated deed transferring the property to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or a third-party purchaser
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the resale restrictions must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In compliance with all federal, State and local laws, rules and regulations, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Stated in a separate covenant, restriction, easement, or condition in a deed or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: instrument executed by or on behalf of the owner of the land or property and recorded against
elements.label: text

elements.text: that land or property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The resale restrictions may be administered by a subsidy provider or program administrator.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers must meet any program eligibility requirements established by the subsidy provider or
elements.label: text

elements.text: program administrator, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For more information on requirements regarding foreclosure redemption periods, see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9301.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: for Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions (12/04/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable, recurring monthly payments associated with the financial subsidy that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided when the property subject to resale restrictions was purchased by the Borrower must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the monthly housing expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any term included in the resale restricted covenants requiring the owner of a property subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resale restrictions to make payments under certain circumstances or requiring repayment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial subsidy, in whole or in part, must state that the payment obligation is subordinate to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: First Lien Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406.4: Appraisal requirements for Mortgages secured
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: by properties subject to resale restrictions (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal report conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal reports when resale restrictions survive foreclosure or recordation of a deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal reports when resale restrictions terminate upon foreclosure (or expiration of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable legally required foreclosure redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Appraisal report conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For properties subject to resale restrictions, the appraisal report must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note the existence of any resale restrictions, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include an analysis that addresses any impact the resale restrictions have on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property’s value or marketability
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by properties subject to age-based resale restrictions may qualify for
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated collateral evaluation (ACE) or ACE+ PDR. See Section 5602.3 for details
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding ACE or Section 5602.4 for ACE+ PDR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appraisal reports when resale restrictions survive foreclosure or recordation of a deed￾in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the appraisal report must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reflect the impact the resale restrictions have on the subject property’s value, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When available, be supported by comparable sales with similar resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: If recent comparable sales with similar resale restrictions are not available in the Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area, the appraiser should:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Use similarly restricted older comparable sales from the Market Area, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Consider recent and older similarly restricted sales from competing Market Areas as
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales or as supporting market data
elements.label: text

elements.text: When comparable sales with similar resale restrictions are not available, as long as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser can justify and support their use in the appraisal report, the appraiser may use
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ With different resale restrictions, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Without resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Appraisal reports when resale restrictions terminate upon foreclosure (or expiration of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any applicable legally required foreclosure redemption period) or recordation of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions that terminate upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required foreclosure redemption period)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the appraisal report must reflect the market
elements.label: text

elements.text: value of the property without resale restrictions by using comparable sales that are not resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller, or any third party authorized by the Seller, must ensure that the Borrower and
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser are aware of the resale restrictions and must advise the appraiser that he or she must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the following statement in the appraisal report:
elements.label: text

elements.text: “This appraisal is made on the basis of a hypothetical condition that the property rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: being appraised are without resale and other restrictions that are terminated
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically upon the latter of foreclosure or the expiration of any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption period, or upon recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406.6: Documentation options and public land
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: records requirements for Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties subject to resale restrictions (12/04/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Public land records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Documentation options
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac allows the optional use of the Grounded Solutions Network (GSN) 2021 Model
elements.label: text

elements.text: Declaration of Affordability Covenants with Refinance and Resale Restriction and Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Option and 2021 Model Subordinate Program Mortgage. Collectively, these documents are
elements.label: text

elements.text: referred to as the “Model Declaration.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: GSN also provides Commentary on the 2021 Model Declaration of Affordability Covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Refinance and Resale Restriction and Purchase Option, which may be found here. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Model Declaration was developed to promote best practices and standardization for deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted affordable housing programs while also providing flexibility in program design.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Model Declaration documents may be found here.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Public land records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the resale restrictions must appear in the public land records for the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises in a manner discoverable by a routine title search.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406.7: Right of first refusal requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions (12/04/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions, any right of first refusal must
elements.label: text

elements.text: run to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The enabling authority or jurisdiction that imposed the resale restrictions, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subsidy provider or program administrator
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Mortgage is subject to foreclosure proceedings and/or an approved short sale, the right
elements.label: text

elements.text: of first refusal may not continue beyond 120 days after the date of written notice that the resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted property is being offered for sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any right of first refusal may not adversely impact the rights of Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer to foreclose or acquire title to the resale restricted property, accept a deed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment in lieu of foreclosure, or transfer title to or lease the resale restricted property, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquired.

elements.text: 4406.8: Mortgages secured by properties subject to
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: income-based resale restrictions (12/04/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income-based resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Down Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Income-based resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable housing programs use income-based resale restrictions to create affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing opportunities. These affordable housing programs are often based on State or local
elements.label: text

elements.text: inclusionary housing policies, which typically require a specified number or percentage of
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties in a designated area to be dedicated as housing for individuals and households
elements.label: text

elements.text: with very low-, low- or moderate-incomes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income-based resale restrictions restrict the initial sales price and subsequent resale price
elements.label: text

elements.text: of properties subject to such resale restrictions. In doing so, the resale restricted price
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides a form of subsidy to the homebuyer in an amount equal to the difference between
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sales price and the market value of the property without resale restrictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage secured by a property subject to income-based resale restrictions may be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase transaction, “no cash-out” or cash-out refinance Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: “No cash-out” and cash-out refinance Mortgages are permitted with prior written approval of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transaction from the subsidy provider or program administrator. The Mortgage file must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain evidence of such approval and, if applicable, the approved amount of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds that the Borrower may receive.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage proceeds from a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage may be used to pay the unpaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal amount used to subsidize the sales price at the time the Borrower purchased the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property subject to income-based resale restrictions and any required share of appreciation
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to the subsidy provider per the resale restricted covenants.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises must be a 1- or 2-unit Primary Residence that is not a Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home, unless the Manufactured Home is a CHOICEHome®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers must meet the program eligibility requirements established by the subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider or program administrator.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions, when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: First Lien Mortgage is a Home Possible® Mortgage or Refi Possible® Mortgage, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must use the Home Possible or Refi Possible income limits, as applicable, to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower eligibility even if the subsidy provider’s or program administrator’s limits are
elements.label: text

elements.text: different.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Down Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions, the Down
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment is based on the resale restricted price.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406.9: Excess proceeds for Mortgages secured by
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: properties subject to income-based resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The subsidy provider may be entitled to any applicable excess proceeds when there is a transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title on a property subject to income-based resale restrictions that occurs as a result of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: property being sold by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower for an amount exceeding the resale restricted price, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac after acquiring title through a completed foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excess proceeds on properties subject to income-based resale restrictions are those proceeds that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are above the amount required to satisfy the total indebtedness, including any additional liens,
elements.label: text

elements.text: claims or encumbrances, in addition to any amount(s) incurred during an REO holding period if
elements.label: text

elements.text: title was acquired by Freddie Mac via a completed foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon satisfaction of the total indebtedness as outlined above, any excess proceeds should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: distributed as outlined below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For properties that are sold by the Borrower for an amount exceeding the resale restricted
elements.label: text

elements.text: price agreed upon in the resale restricted covenants:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First to the Borrower and subsidy provider for equity due as agreed upon in the resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restricted covenants, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All remaining excess proceeds to the subsidy provider when the resale restricted
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants include terms for excess proceeds to be distributed to the subsidy provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For properties that are sold by Freddie Mac after acquiring title through a completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ First to Freddie Mac for any amount(s) incurred during an REO holding period, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All remaining excess proceeds to the subsidy provider when the resale restricted
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants include terms for excess proceeds to be distributed to the subsidy provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Servicers will not be responsible for these REO activities. For more information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer responsibilities on REO properties, see Chapter 9601.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4406.10: Requirements for Affordable Seconds®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: subsidy providers and program administrators for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by properties subject to income￾based resale restrictions (05/07/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Affordable Seconds®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsidy provider as the seller of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-profit entities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsidy providers
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Affordable Seconds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions with Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seconds used to subsidize the sales price of such properties must comply with the Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seconds requirements of Section 4204.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the proceeds of an Affordable Second are used to subsidize the property’s sales price and,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a result, the secondary financing or financial assistance program imposes income-based
elements.label: text

elements.text: resale restrictions on the property, the difference between the market sales price and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resale restricted price represents the subsidy amount provided by the Affordable Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the Affordable Second must not restrict Freddie Mac’s ability to sell or transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises if Freddie Mac acquires title to the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Subsidy provider as the seller of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: A subsidy provider and/or program administrator may also be the property seller or act as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property seller on behalf of a government agency. They are not considered interested parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the requirements for interested party contributions in Section 5501.6 do not apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Non-profit entities
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a subsidy provider is a non-profit entity, the Mortgage file must contain the Internal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Revenue Code 501(c) determination letter that allows for federal tax exemption of the non￾profit entity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Subsidy providers
elements.label: text

elements.text: By delivery of a Mortgage secured by a property subject to income-based resale restrictions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer represents and warrants that the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subsidy provider is, or is managed by, or is housed within, a State or local
elements.label: text

elements.text: government, a government sponsored program or a non-profit corporation that is legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: chartered in the State in which it is located and has a 501(c)3 tax exemption from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS. The subsidy provider may employ a third-party non-profit or, as allowed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable jurisdiction, a for-profit corporation, as a program administrator to manage the
elements.label: text

elements.text: affordable housing program, its income-based resale restrictions and controls.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The income-based resale restrictions are imposed by State or local governments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: municipalities or non-profit entities, to create and preserve affordable housing (including
elements.label: text

elements.text: entities administering governmental sponsored subsidy programs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subsidy provider or program administrator provides home counseling services or has
elements.label: text

elements.text: established partnerships with at least one organization that does
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4407.1: Eligibility of properties impacted by a disaster
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condition of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that each property securing a Mortgage sold to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac meets the property eligibility requirements in the Purchase Documents, including, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, the collateral representation and warranty requirements regarding the value,
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition and marketability of the Mortgaged Premises in Section 5602.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have policies and procedures in place to assess whether there has been any
elements.label: text

elements.text: adverse effect on the Mortgaged Premises that needs to be addressed before the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for delivery to Freddie Mac. If the Mortgage is a Condominium Unit Mortgage, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s assessment should include damage to Condominium Project Common Elements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate from any specific Condominium Unit damage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condition of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage secured by a property impacted by a disaster to be eligible for sale to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property damage must not have impacted the safety, soundness or structural integrity
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must ensure all damage is documented and is covered by insurance as required
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Chapter 4703
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property securing the Mortgage has been damaged such that the damage impacts the
elements.label: text

elements.text: safety, soundness or structural integrity of the Mortgaged Premises, the property is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable as security for the Mortgage, and the Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac until all repairs to the property are documented and completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4408: Mortgages Made Pursuant to
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Employee Relocation Programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4408.1: Mortgages made pursuant to employee
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: relocation programs (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The provisions of this chapter apply to Mortgages made pursuant to an employee relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: program. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special pooling and delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: These Mortgages must be made to a newly hired or transferred employee to finance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase of a 1- to 4-unit Primary Residence at a new job location pursuant to an employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation program that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establishes the terms and conditions under which the employer relocates employees, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is administered by the employer or its agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are ineligible Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Caution Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with capitalized balances as described in Chapter 4403
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage is considered to be secured by a Primary Residence when the Borrower occupies
elements.label: text

elements.text: all or part of the Mortgaged Premises as a Primary Residence no later than 180 days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date or the Effective Date of Permanent Financing for Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages. If the occupancy of the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: delayed more than 90 days, the Seller must maintain in the Mortgage file a signed statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Borrower confirming their intent to occupy the property within 180 days of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date or the Effective Date of Permanent Financing for Construction to Permanent Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Renovation Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Underwriting methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Accept Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Manually Underwritten Mortgage, if the Mortgage was:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Never submitted to Loan Product Advisor® or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Submitted to Loan Product Advisor and did not receive a Risk Class
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Establishing Borrower credit reputation with foreign credit references for Manually
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manually Underwritten Mortgages, when the Borrower does not have the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: required number of payment references established in the United States as required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5202.1(a)(i), or when the Borrower does not have a usable Credit Score, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller may determine that the Borrower has established an acceptable credit reputation in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a foreign country if all of the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The determination is based on a minimum of three Tradelines. (Noncredit Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: References established outside of the United States are not eligible.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage file contains a credit report meeting the requirements of Section 5203.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: and confirming that the Borrower does not have a sufficient number of payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: references established in the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Current Primary Residence pending sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s current Primary Residence is pending sale and the sale will not close
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Note Date of the Mortgage (or, for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan purpose for Home Possible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Home Possible Mortgage must be either a purchase transaction or “no cash-out” refinance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible Mortgages eligible for sale to Freddie Mac must be First Lien Mortgages that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are fully amortizing and must be one of the following conventional Mortgage Products:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fixed-rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 7/6-Month or 10/6-Month ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 5/6-Month ARMs if secured by a property other than a Manufactured Home

elements.text: Home Possible Mortgages, other than Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have an original maturity date not greater than 30 years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes must have a maximum original
elements.label: text

elements.text: maturity not greater than that specified in Section 5703.8(a).
